extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon attacked on the final 15% ramp to Lagos de Covadonga, dropped QUINTANA who hesitated tactically, taking stage and red jersey
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA Nairo fatally waited for LOPEZ to lead rather than attacking himself when gradient hit 15%, losing both stage and time
YATES Simon's instinctive first attack when gradient steepened forced rivals to chase rather than organize, creating gaps when they looked back
VALVERDE Alejandro marked wheels throughout steep section then launched final hairpin sprint, gaining seconds on hesitant QUINTANA despite not winning
LOPEZ Miguel Angel only attacked when prompted by others rather than initiating, revealing he'll struggle in head-to-head GC battles

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: attacked instinctively when gradient hit maximum steepness, suggesting improved Lagos climbing form versus his previous vulnerability
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: strategically passive on decisive steep gradient, waiting for LOPEZ to lead rather than imposing himself despite needing time
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: neutral: strong enough to stay with leaders but consistently reactive, never initiating attacks on steepest gradients
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: accelerated through final hairpin when others faded, showing superior technical descending/flat sprint skills after steep climbing
rider_signals.HERRADA Jesus: negative: lost contact with lead group on 15% section, disappointing for breakaway contender
